headline: Electronic Arts launches leveraged loan backing largest LBO debt deal since financial crisis
summary: Electronic Arts officially kicked off marketing for the debt financing backing its LBO with a launch on March 16 of the cross-border loan deal totaling $5.75 billion. It is part of the largest LBO debt-financing package since the Global Financial Crisis. In addition to the $4 billion and €1.531 billion ($1.75 billion) term loan B tranches launched today, financing for the deal will include a $3.25 billion term loan A, $6.5 billion of other dollar and euro secured debt, and $2.5 billion of unsecu

headline: Electronic Arts $15 billion debt sale draws $25 billion demand
summary: Investors have placed about $9 billion of orders for a roughly $4 billion leveraged loan sale tied to the deal that a JPMorgan Chase & Co.-led bank group launched earlier this week, according to people with knowledge of the matter.  There’s also growing interest in the junk-bond portion of the financing, which is expected to hit the market as soon as next week.  Demand for a $4.75 billion secured bond has reached about $9 billion, said the people, who asked not to be identified because they’re not authorized to speak publicly.
